specific_location: Greenhorn Creek diversion gate about .5 mile west of milepost 32, CSH 165.
description: This bird was about same size & shape as nearby Ash-throated Flycatchers, but with a smaller more rounded head.  Head, throat, back, and wings were solid black except for small but well defined white patch at base of primaries on bottom of folded wing. This patch appeared nearly square shaped. I didn't see the usual wisps of white linings sometimes seen at close range nor any white in otherwise longish dark tail, probably because I was about 50 yards from the bird.  Since bird was facing away from me, I only saw its backside with glimpses of orange flanks when it occasionally turned slightly to the right.Behaviors: Bird sat atop narrow-leaf cottonwood looking about and occasionally preening before flying off.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
